comments.rawMarkdown:
Here are the versions we're now using (all of which are pinned):

```python
pip install numpy==2.4.4
pip install onnx==1.21.0
pip install onnxruntime==1.24.4
pip install onnx-tool==1.0.1
```

Depending on what happens with `onnx-tool`, we may need to upgrade one more time (but are hoping to settle on the final version ASAP).

comments.rawMarkdown:
I'm probing the judge environment by submitting models one at a time, but with only 5 submissions per day, using submissions for debugging is extremely painful.

**I would really like a `pyproject.toml` (or better yet, a `Dockerfile`) that reproduces the judge's environment.**

I would also like a **firm commitment that versions will remain pinned, even as new releases of `onnx` / `onnxruntime` come out**. I understand that the judge itself or `onnx-tool` may need to change to address some issues, but the versions of the underlying platform that actually runs the submitted models should be pinned. The techniques that are usable differ across ONNX opset versions, so pinning is the appropriate setup for a code-golf competition. In fact, the previous Python code-golf competition pinned the Python version.

![](https://www.googleapis.com/download/storage/v1/b/kaggle-forum-message-attachments/o/inbox%2F1953252%2F912355b6758f3ac3d0b2965c191e3246%2F2026-04-20%20092722.png?generation=1776645363049411&alt=media)

comments.rawMarkdown: I used all my submissions today to try to figure out the error.  I confirmed 100% solution to 334 was producing errors for me, and I suspect 167 also.   AI "fixed" my solution by writing a better scoring look-up table (and thus causing it to crash).  Maybe that helps some of you.

comments.rawMarkdown:
I am also curious about this.

Specifically, while onnx-tool has had several bug fixes since the competition started, it seems they have not been reflected in the evaluation environment. This issue undermines the credibility of the Longest Leader, as it allows scores to be unfairly and easily inflated. (I had to drop my score by about 200 points when I updated my local evaluation environment from v0.9.0 to v1.0.1.)

Could you please look into this? @mmoffitt

firstMessage.rawMarkdown:
Dear Host ,
Could you kindly clarify the exact ONNX compatibility stack used in the competition scoring environment?

I’m specifically trying to confirm:
- the `onnx` version
- the `onnxruntime` version
- the maximum supported ONNX `ir_version`
- the supported ONNX `opset` range used during validation/scoring

I’m seeing some models fail even though they appear structurally valid in newer local environments. For example, a few models exported as `IR 13 / opset 12` fail to load in my local validation path, so I want to confirm the official competition limits rather than guess.

I understand that the following ONNX features/operations are disallowed: `Loop`, `Scan`, `NonZero`, `Unique`, `Script`, and `Function`. However, it also seems that some other operators may not work reliably in the actual scoring/runtime environment even if they are not explicitly banned. For example, I suspect `TopK` and possibly some others may fail or behave inconsistently in practice.

Could you clarify whether there is a broader list of operators that are unsupported in the real evaluation environment, beyond the explicitly disallowed set?

For example, one of my local environments is using:

onnx: 1.18.0
onnxruntime: 1.22.0

In that environment:

onnx.checker only supports up to IR version 11
local onnxruntime only supports up to IR version 10

But my another environment exported the model with IR 13 / opset 12.
and Kaggle env : py=3.12.12|onnx=1.20.1|ort=1.24.4|onnx_tool=unknown

So an older local stack may fail to load an ONNX model generated by a newer stack, because the model format is too new for the older runtime.

**A precise compatibility list would help avoid submission failures caused by exporter/runtime mismatches.**

Thanks.
firstMessage.content:
Dear Host , Could you kindly clarify the exact ONNX compatibility stack used in the competition scoring environment?

I’m specifically trying to confirm:

  • the onnx version
  • the onnxruntime version
  • the maximum supported ONNX ir_version
  • the supported ONNX opset range used during validation/scoring

I’m seeing some models fail even though they appear structurally valid in newer local environments. For example, a few models exported as IR 13 / opset 12 fail to load in my local validation path, so I want to confirm the official competition limits rather than guess.

I understand that the following ONNX features/operations are disallowed: Loop, Scan, NonZero, Unique, Script, and Function. However, it also seems that some other operators may not work reliably in the actual scoring/runtime environment even if they are not explicitly banned. For example, I suspect TopK and possibly some others may fail or behave inconsistently in practice.

Could you clarify whether there is a broader list of operators that are unsupported in the real evaluation environment, beyond the explicitly disallowed set?

For example, one of my local environments is using:

onnx: 1.18.0 onnxruntime: 1.22.0

In that environment:

onnx.checker only supports up to IR version 11 local onnxruntime only supports up to IR version 10

But my another environment exported the model with IR 13 / opset 12. and Kaggle env : py=3.12.12|onnx=1.20.1|ort=1.24.4|onnx_tool=unknown

So an older local stack may fail to load an ONNX model generated by a newer stack, because the model format is too new for the older runtime.

A precise compatibility list would help avoid submission failures caused by exporter/runtime mismatches.

Thanks.

forumTopic.name: Question About ONNX Runtime Compatibility in the Scoring Environment(target IR version and opset)
